data.title: Quadient (ENXTPA:QDT) EPS Reset In H1 2026 Tests Profitability Improvement Narrative
data.description:
Quadient (ENXTPA:QDT) has opened FY 2026 with first half revenue of €517 million and basic EPS of €0.60, setting the tone for how its transition phase is feeding through to the income statement. Over the recent reporting periods, the company has seen revenue move from €534 million in H1 2025 to €558.6 million in H2 2025, with EPS shifting from €0.74 to €1.20 over the same stretch, while trailing 12 month EPS for H1 2026 sits at €1.80. The latest print gives investors a fresh read on how margins are holding up. Against that backdrop, the key question is whether these results point to a business gradually tightening its margins or one still working through pressure on profitability.

See our full analysis for Quadient.

With the headline numbers on the table, the next step is to see how they line up with the most common narratives around Quadient's growth potential, risk profile, and path back to stronger profitability.

See what the community is saying about Quadient

Advertisement

Trailing 12 month loss despite €61.9 million half year profit

  • Over the last 12 months, Quadient shows a trailing loss of €68 million even though H1 2026 on its own reports €20.6 million of net income and trailing 12 month net income of €61.9 million, so the earlier part of the 12 month window contains heavier losses than the latest half.
  • Consensus narrative highlights improving profit margins from 6.1% to an expected 8.5%, and this earnings reset sits awkwardly against the current loss, because:
    • Analysts see earnings rising to €96.7 million, yet the trailing 12 month loss means the company still needs to close a sizeable gap between where profits are today and those expectations.
    • H2 2025 net income of €41.3 million versus H1 2026 net income of €20.6 million shows profitability moving around between half years, which makes a smooth margin path less certain than the consensus margin line suggests.

Revenue at €1.1b with modest 1.5% growth forecast

  • On a trailing basis, revenue sits around €1.1b, with analysts expecting only 1.5% annual revenue growth compared with 5.5% for the wider French market, so the business is modeled as growing more slowly than the market overall.
  • Bulls argue that the push into subscription and SaaS can support stronger growth, yet the current numbers pull in the other direction, because:
    • Trailing 12 month revenue has been near €1.1b across recent periods, which aligns more with a steady profile than with a step up that a stronger bullish growth case might imply.
    • The bullish narrative talks about revenue growth of 2.6% a year, which is higher than the 1.5% consensus forecast and suggests some investors are baking in more uplift than the base case currently assumes.
Bulls point to subscription growth as a future engine, but the modest 1.5% revenue growth forecast gives you a useful benchmark for judging how ambitious those expectations are when you read the 🐂 Quadient Bull Case.

DCF fair value above price while losses and weak interest cover persist

  • Quadient trades at a P/S of 0.4x against peers at 4.4x and the European Tech industry at 0.7x, and a DCF fair value of about €15.97 sits above the current €10.88 share price, even though trailing 12 month results still show a €68 million loss and interest payments are not well covered by earnings.
  • Bears focus on those financing pressures and Mail headwinds, and the figures here give that cautious view plenty to work with, because:
    • Losses have been increasing at about 11.2% a year over the past five years, so the current trailing loss is not an isolated blip but part of a multi year pattern in the data.
    • Mail revenue is reported to be in organic decline and interest coverage is weak, so a low P/S and a DCF fair value above the market price come alongside ongoing earnings and balance sheet risks rather than after they have been resolved.
If you are weighing that low P/S and DCF fair value against the ongoing loss and interest coverage issues, it is worth seeing how skeptics frame the Mail decline and financing risk in the 🐻 Quadient Bear Case.

Next Steps

Mixed messages on growth, margins, and valuation can be confusing. Take a moment to weigh the numbers and sentiment yourself, then check the 3 key rewards and 2 important warning signs.

data.title: Has The Recent 17.5% Pullback In SPX Technologies (SPXC) Opened A New Opportunity?
data.description:
• Investors may be wondering whether SPX Technologies at around US$200 a share still offers value, or if most of the upside has already been priced in.
  • The stock has recently seen a 17.5% decline over the last 30 days, even though the 1 year return sits at 50.0% and the 3 year return is 185.7%.
  • Recent coverage has focused on SPX Technologies as a capital goods name that has attracted attention after strong multi year share price performance, which sets expectations high for what comes next. At the same time, the recent pullback has raised questions about whether sentiment has cooled or whether this is just a pause after a long run.
  • Currently, the company scores 2/6 on Simply Wall St's valuation checks. The next sections will walk through what different valuation approaches say about SPX Technologies and will outline a broader way of judging value that ties everything together by the end of the article.

SPX Technologies scores just 2/6 on our valuation checks. See what other red flags we found in the full valuation breakdown.

Advertisement

Approach 1: SPX Technologies Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model estimates what a company might be worth today by projecting its future cash flows and then discounting those back to a single present value.

For SPX Technologies, the model uses last twelve month Free Cash Flow of about $275.1 million and projects how this cash flow could evolve over time. Analyst estimates are available out to 2028, with Free Cash Flow for that year projected at $476.05 million. Beyond that, Simply Wall St extrapolates additional annual Free Cash Flow figures out to 2035 using the same framework.

Using these cash flow projections within a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity model, the DCF estimate for SPX Technologies comes out at an intrinsic value of roughly $204.85 per share. Compared with a share price around $200, the model suggests the stock trades at about a 2.1% discount, which is a small gap and well within a reasonable margin of error.

Result: ABOUT RIGHT

SPX Technologies is fairly valued according to our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF), but this can change at a moment's notice. Track the value in your watchlist or portfolio and be alerted on when to act.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for SPX Technologies.

Approach 2: SPX Technologies Price vs Earnings

For profitable companies, the P/E ratio is a useful way to relate what you pay for each share to the earnings that support that share price. It helps you see how many dollars of price the market is assigning to each dollar of earnings.

What counts as a “normal” P/E depends on how fast earnings are expected to grow and how risky those earnings are. Higher growth or lower perceived risk can justify a higher multiple, while slower growth or higher risk typically points to a lower one.

SPX Technologies currently trades on a P/E of 40.7x, compared with a Machinery industry average of about 27.0x and a peer group average of 27.6x. Simply Wall St also provides a proprietary “Fair Ratio” of 31.6x, which estimates what P/E might be appropriate after considering factors such as earnings growth, margins, industry, market cap and risk profile.

This Fair Ratio can be more useful than a simple peer or industry comparison because it adjusts for the specific characteristics of SPX Technologies rather than assuming all companies deserve the same multiple. With the current 40.7x P/E sitting above the 31.6x Fair Ratio, the shares screen as somewhat expensive on this metric.

Narratives are introduced here as a simple way for you to attach a clear story to your numbers by linking your view of SPX Technologies, including assumptions about future revenue, earnings, margins and a fair value estimate, to a live forecast on Simply Wall St's Community page. You can then compare that fair value to the current price to decide whether the stock looks attractive or stretched. Each Narrative updates automatically when new news or earnings arrive. data.title: Saftec Co.,Ltd. (TSE:7464) Pays A JP¥60.00 Dividend In Just Two Days
data.description:
It looks like Saftec Co.,Ltd. (TSE:7464) is about to go ex-dividend in the next 2 days. The ex-dividend date is commonly two business days before the record date, which is the cut-off date for shareholders to be present on the company's books to be eligible for a dividend payment. It is important to be aware of the ex-dividend date because any trade on the stock needs to have been settled on or before the record date. Accordingly, SaftecLtd investors that purchase the stock on or after the 30th of March will not receive the dividend, which will be paid on the 30th of June.

The company's next dividend payment will be JP¥60.00 per share. Last year, in total, the company distributed JP¥60.00 to shareholders. Based on the last year's worth of payments, SaftecLtd has a trailing yield of 3.7% on the current stock price of JP¥1641.00. Dividends are a major contributor to investment returns for long term holders, but only if the dividend continues to be paid. As a result, readers should always check whether SaftecLtd has been able to grow its dividends, or if the dividend might be cut.

We've found 21 US stocks that are forecast to pay a dividend yield of over 6% next year. See the full list for free.

Dividends are typically paid out of company income, so if a company pays out more than it earned, its dividend is usually at a higher risk of being cut. SaftecLtd paid out more than half (69%) of its earnings last year, which is a regular payout ratio for most companies. Yet cash flows are even more important than profits for assessing a dividend, so we need to see if the company generated enough cash to pay its distribution. What's good is that dividends were well covered by free cash flow, with the company paying out 16% of its cash flow last year.

It's positive to see that SaftecLtd's dividend is covered by both profits and cash flow, since this is generally a sign that the dividend is sustainable, and a lower payout ratio usually suggests a greater margin of safety before the dividend gets cut.

Check out our latest analysis for SaftecLtd

Click here to see how much of its profit SaftecLtd paid out over the last 12 months.

Advertisement

Have Earnings And Dividends Been Growing?

When earnings decline, dividend companies become much harder to analyse and own safely. If business enters a downturn and the dividend is cut, the company could see its value fall precipitously. With that in mind, we're discomforted by SaftecLtd's 17% per annum decline in earnings in the past five years. Ultimately, when earnings per share decline, the size of the pie from which dividends can be paid, shrinks.

The main way most investors will assess a company's dividend prospects is by checking the historical rate of dividend growth. In the last 10 years, SaftecLtd has lifted its dividend by approximately 5.5% a year on average. That's interesting, but the combination of a growing dividend despite declining earnings can typically only be achieved by paying out more of the company's profits. This can be valuable for shareholders, but it can't go on forever.

Final Takeaway

Should investors buy SaftecLtd for the upcoming dividend? We're not enthused by the declining earnings per share, although at least the company's payout ratio is within a reasonable range, meaning it may not be at imminent risk of a dividend cut. Overall, it's not a bad combination, but we feel that there are likely more attractive dividend prospects out there.

So if you want to do more digging on SaftecLtd, you'll find it worthwhile knowing the risks that this stock faces. Every company has risks, and we've spotted 3 warning signs for SaftecLtd (of which 1 is significant!) you should know about.

data.title: Don't Buy ES-CON JAPAN Ltd. (TSE:8892) For Its Next Dividend Without Doing These Checks
data.description:
Some investors rely on dividends for growing their wealth, and if you're one of those dividend sleuths, you might be intrigued to know that ES-CON JAPAN Ltd. (TSE:8892) is about to go ex-dividend in just 2 days. The ex-dividend date is two business days before a company's record date in most cases, which is the date on which the company determines which shareholders are entitled to receive a dividend. The ex-dividend date is important because any transaction on a stock needs to have been settled before the record date in order to be eligible for a dividend. Thus, you can purchase ES-CON JAPAN's shares before the 30th of March in order to receive the dividend, which the company will pay on the 26th of June.

The company's next dividend payment will be JP¥48.00 per share, and in the last 12 months, the company paid a total of JP¥48.00 per share. Last year's total dividend payments show that ES-CON JAPAN has a trailing yield of 4.2% on the current share price of JP¥1137.00. We love seeing companies pay a dividend, but it's also important to be sure that laying the golden eggs isn't going to kill our golden goose! As a result, readers should always check whether ES-CON JAPAN has been able to grow its dividends, or if the dividend might be cut.

We've found 21 US stocks that are forecast to pay a dividend yield of over 6% next year. See the full list for free.

Dividends are typically paid out of company income, so if a company pays out more than it earned, its dividend is usually at a higher risk of being cut. ES-CON JAPAN paid out more than half (52%) of its earnings last year, which is a regular payout ratio for most companies. Yet cash flow is typically more important than profit for assessing dividend sustainability, so we should always check if the company generated enough cash to afford its dividend. It paid out an unsustainably high 228% of its free cash flow as dividends over the past 12 months, which is worrying. It's pretty hard to pay out more than you earn, so we wonder how ES-CON JAPAN intends to continue funding this dividend, or if it could be forced to cut the payment.

While ES-CON JAPAN's dividends were covered by the company's reported profits, cash is somewhat more important, so it's not great to see that the company didn't generate enough cash to pay its dividend. Were this to happen repeatedly, this would be a risk to ES-CON JAPAN's ability to maintain its dividend.

See our latest analysis for ES-CON JAPAN

Click here to see how much of its profit ES-CON JAPAN paid out over the last 12 months.

Advertisement

Have Earnings And Dividends Been Growing?

Businesses with shrinking earnings are tricky from a dividend perspective. If business enters a downturn and the dividend is cut, the company could see its value fall precipitously. Readers will understand then, why we're concerned to see ES-CON JAPAN's earnings per share have dropped 13% a year over the past five years. When earnings per share fall, the maximum amount of dividends that can be paid also falls.

The main way most investors will assess a company's dividend prospects is by checking the historical rate of dividend growth. In the past 10 years, ES-CON JAPAN has increased its dividend at approximately 15% a year on average. Growing the dividend payout ratio while earnings are declining can deliver nice returns for a while, but it's always worth checking for when the company can't increase the payout ratio any more - because then the music stops.

Final Takeaway

Should investors buy ES-CON JAPAN for the upcoming dividend? It's definitely not great to see earnings per share shrinking. The company paid out an acceptable percentage of its income, but an uncomfortably high percentage of its cash flow over the past year. With the way things are shaping up from a dividend perspective, we'd be inclined to steer clear of ES-CON JAPAN.

With that in mind though, if the poor dividend characteristics of ES-CON JAPAN don't faze you, it's worth being mindful of the risks involved with this business. Our analysis shows 2 warning signs for ES-CON JAPAN that we strongly recommend you have a look at before investing in the company.

Regular readers will know that we love our dividends at Simply Wall St, which is why it's exciting to see Cybertrust Japan Co., Ltd. (TSE:4498) is about to trade ex-dividend in the next 2 days. The ex-dividend date is commonly two business days before the record date, which is the cut-off date for shareholders to be present on the company's books to be eligible for a dividend payment. The ex-dividend date is important because any transaction on a stock needs to have been settled before the record date in order to be eligible for a dividend. In other words, investors can purchase Cybertrust Japan's shares before the 30th of March in order to be eligible for the dividend, which will be paid on the 30th of June.

The company's upcoming dividend is JP¥11.50 a share, following on from the last 12 months, when the company distributed a total of JP¥11.50 per share to shareholders. Calculating the last year's worth of payments shows that Cybertrust Japan has a trailing yield of 1.0% on the current share price of JP¥1171.00. We love seeing companies pay a dividend, but it's also important to be sure that laying the golden eggs isn't going to kill our golden goose! So we need to investigate whether Cybertrust Japan can afford its dividend, and if the dividend could grow.

This technology could replace computers: discover the 20 stocks are working to make quantum computing a reality.

Dividends are typically paid out of company income, so if a company pays out more than it earned, its dividend is usually at a higher risk of being cut. Cybertrust Japan has a low and conservative payout ratio of just 20% of its income after tax. Yet cash flows are even more important than profits for assessing a dividend, so we need to see if the company generated enough cash to pay its distribution. What's good is that dividends were well covered by free cash flow, with the company paying out 20% of its cash flow last year.

It's positive to see that Cybertrust Japan's dividend is covered by both profits and cash flow, since this is generally a sign that the dividend is sustainable, and a lower payout ratio usually suggests a greater margin of safety before the dividend gets cut.

Have Earnings And Dividends Been Growing?

Businesses with strong growth prospects usually make the best dividend payers, because it's easier to grow dividends when earnings per share are improving. If business enters a downturn and the dividend is cut, the company could see its value fall precipitously. Fortunately for readers, Cybertrust Japan's earnings per share have been growing at 20% a year for the past five years. Earnings per share are growing rapidly and the company is keeping more than half of its earnings within the business; an attractive combination which could suggest the company is focused on reinvesting to grow earnings further. This will make it easier to fund future growth efforts and we think this is an attractive combination - plus the dividend can always be increased later.

The main way most investors will assess a company's dividend prospects is by checking the historical rate of dividend growth. Cybertrust Japan has delivered 9.5% dividend growth per year on average over the past three years. It's encouraging to see the company lifting dividends while earnings are growing, suggesting at least some corporate interest in rewarding shareholders.

Final Takeaway

From a dividend perspective, should investors buy or avoid Cybertrust Japan? We love that Cybertrust Japan is growing earnings per share while simultaneously paying out a low percentage of both its earnings and cash flow. These characteristics suggest the company is reinvesting in growing its business, while the conservative payout ratio also implies a reduced risk of the dividend being cut in the future. It's a promising combination that should mark this company worthy of closer attention.

data.title: Green Tea Group Limited Just Recorded A 5.9% EPS Beat: Here's What Analysts Are Forecasting Next
data.description:
Investors in Green Tea Group Limited (HKG:6831) had a good week, as its shares rose 4.1% to close at HK$7.90 following the release of its annual results. The result was positive overall - although revenues of CN¥4.8b were in line with what the analysts predicted, Green Tea Group surprised by delivering a statutory profit of CN¥0.80 per share, modestly greater than expected. Following the result, the analysts have updated their earnings model, and it would be good to know whether they think there's been a strong change in the company's prospects, or if it's business as usual. So we collected the latest post-earnings statutory consensus estimates to see what could be in store for next year.

Trump has pledged to "unleash" American oil and gas and these 15 US stocks have developments that are poised to benefit.

Following the latest results, Green Tea Group's six analysts are now forecasting revenues of CN¥5.88b in 2026. This would be a major 24% improvement in revenue compared to the last 12 months. Statutory earnings per share are predicted to bounce 35% to CN¥0.98. Before this earnings report, the analysts had been forecasting revenues of CN¥5.83b and earnings per share (EPS) of CN¥0.86 in 2026. Although the revenue estimates have not really changed, we can see there's been a substantial gain in earnings per share expectations, suggesting that the analysts have become more bullish after the latest result.

View our latest analysis for Green Tea Group

The consensus price target rose 5.7% to HK$12.12, suggesting that higher earnings estimates flow through to the stock's valuation as well. There's another way to think about price targets though, and that's to look at the range of price targets put forward by analysts, because a wide range of estimates could suggest a diverse view on possible outcomes for the business. The most optimistic Green Tea Group analyst has a price target of HK$13.36 per share, while the most pessimistic values it at HK$11.00. The narrow spread of estimates could suggest that the business' future is relatively easy to value, or thatthe analysts have a strong view on its prospects.

Of course, another way to look at these forecasts is to place them into context against the industry itself. It's clear from the latest estimates that Green Tea Group's rate of growth is expected to accelerate meaningfully, with the forecast 24% annualised revenue growth to the end of 2026 noticeably faster than its historical growth of 19% p.a. over the past three years. Compare this with other companies in the same industry, which are forecast to grow their revenue 9.1% annually. It seems obvious that, while the growth outlook is brighter than the recent past, the analysts also expect Green Tea Group to grow faster than the wider industry.

Advertisement

The Bottom Line

The biggest takeaway for us is the consensus earnings per share upgrade, which suggests a clear improvement in sentiment around Green Tea Group's earnings potential next year. Happily, there were no major changes to revenue forecasts, with the business still expected to grow faster than the wider industry. There was also a nice increase in the price target, with the analysts clearly feeling that the intrinsic value of the business is improving.

Keeping that in mind, we still think that the longer term trajectory of the business is much more important for investors to consider. We have estimates - from multiple Green Tea Group analysts - going out to 2028, and you can see them free on our platform here.

data.title: Bayer Links Canola Program And Ludacris Partnership To Test Growth Story
data.description:
• Bayer launches the Advancing Better Canola (ABCs) initiative focused on collaboration, research and sustainability in Canadian canola production.
  • The company announces a new partnership with Grammy winner Ludacris to front consumer health messaging for its One A Day® vitamins.
  • Both moves highlight Bayer's push to connect agricultural science and consumer health branding under one corporate umbrella.

XTRA:BAYN currently trades at about €38.275 per share, with a 1 year return of 69.6% and a 3 year return of 30.4% decline. Over 5 years, the stock shows a 19.0% decline, indicating a mixed experience for longer term holders alongside more recent strength. These new campaigns arrive as investors weigh Bayer's broader execution across its core segments.

The ABCs initiative and the Ludacris partnership provide fresh company driven touchpoints to watch in both agricultural inputs and consumer health. As these programs roll out, key questions will center on how Bayer translates brand reach and ecosystem partnerships into durable relationships with farmers and end consumers.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Bayer by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Bayer.

3 things going right for Bayer that this headline doesn't cover.

The ABCs initiative and the Ludacris partnership sit on opposite ends of Bayer's portfolio, but both point in the same direction: tighter integration between science, data and branding. In Crop Science, ABCs is about Bayer getting closer to Canadian farmers through genetics, agronomy, crop-protection products and digital tools in one system, rather than selling inputs in isolation. That could help defend share against peers like Corteva and Syngenta if it leads to stickier, farm-level relationships. On the Consumer Health side, putting One A Day alongside a mainstream artist is an attempt to keep the brand relevant in a crowded vitamins aisle that includes players such as P&G and Haleon. For investors, the thread to watch is execution. These campaigns do not change Bayer's litigation, capital structure or core pharma exposure, but they do show the company trying to leverage its scale across business lines, from field level platforms to mass-market health messaging.

Advertisement

How This Fits Into The Bayer Narrative

  • ABCs supports the idea in the narrative that Bayer is leaning on data driven Crop Science solutions and closer customer relationships to improve resilience in that division.
  • The focus on marketing led growth in One A Day could challenge narrative assumptions if increased spend does not translate into stronger Consumer Health profitability.
  • The cross segment branding angle, linking agriculture and consumer health, is not a central part of the existing narrative and may not yet be reflected in how long term earnings quality is discussed.

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story. Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for Bayer to help decide what it is worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ Marketing and partnership costs for both ABCs outreach and the Ludacris campaign could weigh on margins if revenue uplift is limited.
  • ⚠️ ABCs success depends on farmer adoption of Bayer's integrated tools, which may be constrained by regulatory changes in crop protection or competition from rival platforms.
  • 🎁 ABCs could deepen customer loyalty in Canadian canola, supporting volume stability in Crop Science if farmers see value in an integrated genetics and digital package.
  • 🎁 A high profile spokesperson may help One A Day defend shelf space and brand awareness in a crowded vitamin category, supporting Bayer's consumer health positioning.

What To Watch Going Forward

Next, keep an eye on concrete indicators that these campaigns are gaining traction, such as grower participation metrics in ABCs, additional ecosystem partners joining the canola program and any commentary from Bayer on One A Day's category share or brand reach during results calls. It is also worth tracking how frequently management ties these initiatives back to broader goals in Crop Science and Consumer Health, including cost discipline and portfolio focus, so you can judge whether they remain targeted efforts or signal a shift in how Bayer allocates marketing and R&D budgets.

data.title: Is Havas (ENXTAM:HAVAS) Offering Value After Recent Share Price Volatility?
data.description:
• If you are wondering whether Havas is attractively priced today, you are not alone. The stock has been drawing attention from investors who care about what they are paying for each euro of potential value.
  • Over the last year Havas has returned 18.0%, while the shorter term picture is mixed, with a 1.9% gain over 7 days, a 10.6% decline over 30 days, and a 11.5% decline year to date.
  • Recent coverage has focused on how these moves fit into the broader media sector conversation and what they may signal about changing risk perceptions for the company. Commentary has raised questions around whether recent share price volatility is driven more by sentiment than by changes in underlying fundamentals.
  • Havas currently has a value score of 6/6, and the rest of this article will walk through the standard valuation checks behind that score before turning to a more holistic way to think about what the stock might be worth.

Havas delivered 18.0% returns over the last year. See how this stacks up to the rest of the Media industry.

Advertisement

Approach 1: Havas Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow model estimates what a business could be worth by projecting its future cash flows and discounting them back to today using a required rate of return.

For Havas, the model uses a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach, starting from last twelve months free cash flow of about €326.4 million. Analyst projections and subsequent extrapolations point to free cash flow of €274.98 million in 2026, rising through the forecast period to a projected €352.17 million in 2035. Simply Wall St uses analyst estimates where available, then extends the trend to build a 10 year cash flow path.

Discounting those projected cash flows back to today produces an estimated intrinsic value of €72.41 per share. In this model, the current share price sits at a 79.1% discount to that intrinsic value. This suggests that the stock screens as significantly undervalued on this DCF view.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests Havas is undervalued by 79.1%. Track this in your watchlist or portfolio, or discover 229 more high quality undervalued stocks.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for Havas.

Approach 2: Havas Price vs Earnings

P/E is often a useful yardstick for profitable companies because it links what you pay directly to what the business currently earns per share. It also captures how the market weighs those earnings against expectations for future growth and the level of risk investors are willing to accept.

In general, higher growth expectations or lower perceived risk can support a higher P/E, while slower expected growth or higher risk usually line up with a lower, more cautious multiple. That is why simply looking at a single P/E number in isolation rarely tells the full story.

Havas currently trades on a P/E of 7.86x, compared with the Media industry average of 15.15x and a peer group average of 19.27x. Simply Wall St also calculates a proprietary “Fair Ratio” of 10.88x for Havas. This Fair Ratio is designed to capture what might be a reasonable P/E once factors such as earnings growth, profit margins, industry, market cap and company specific risks are taken into account.

Because the Fair Ratio blends these fundamentals, it is often more informative than a simple comparison with peers or the broad industry. With Havas trading at 7.86x against a Fair Ratio of 10.88x, the shares appear undervalued on this earnings based view.

Result: UNDERVALUED
